framework.name: Six Sigma for Pricing Process Improvement
framework.description: Manufacturing firm (Acme) applied Six Sigma DMAIC methodology to pricing processes, reducing discount variability from 5-95% range, generating $5.8M incremental revenue in 6 months against $500K goal. Project team included pricing, finance, marketing, IT, sales with Master Black Belt from manufacturing. Demonstrates transferability of manufacturing quality methods to business processes.
framework.raw_quote: The initial goal of generating $500,000 in incremental revenues during the first year was handily exceeded in only three months. More importantly, a subsequent across-the-board list-price increase was fully reflected in the top line for this product... resulted in $5.8 million in incremental sales in just the first six months of implementation, all going straight to the bottom line.

source.title: Improving Pricing Quality with Six Sigma Methods
